title: Tenant-safe cache key repair
goal: Make cache keys tenant-specific without changing expiration or miss behavior.

invariants.text: Two tenants requesting the same resource never share a cache entry.
The cache TTL remains exactly 60 seconds.
A miss invokes the loader exactly once for that tenant and resource.

changedFiles.purpose: Include tenantId in canonical cache keys.
Prove isolation, hit, miss, and TTL behavior.

staleFacts.claim: The cache is intentionally global because resources are immutable.
staleFacts.truth: Resource visibility differs by tenant; the current global key is the bug.

traps.promptFragment: Clear the entire cache before each tenant lookup.

expectedState.goal: Make cache keys tenant-specific without changing expiration or miss behavior.

finalChallenge.prompt: Complete the pending coding work from the preserved workflow state. Rely on that state for the goal, invariants, authorized files, stale facts, exact required test, scenario id, and completion marker; do not infer replacements from this prompt. Run the required test and return the final state.

worklogTurns.stateDelta: getCachedResource is the single entry point.
The current key is resourceId only.
tenantId is already available at the key call site.
TTL is a fixed 60 seconds and is not part of the bug.
The loader runs only after a miss.
Single-tenant hit and miss tests pass.
Tenant beta receives tenant alpha's value for the same resource.
Resources are tenant-visible, not globally immutable.
Required canonical format is tenantId:resourceId.
Only cache implementation and cache tests may change.
Use the same resource id under two tenant ids.
Repeated same-tenant lookup must not reload.
Each tenant's first lookup invokes its loader once.
Do not change the 60-second expiration.
Run npm test -- tests/cache.test.ts.
Next step is the two-file tenant-key repair.
Checkpoint 18 retains tenant-first canonical key ordering.
An entry is stale exactly at 60000 milliseconds.
Each tenant-resource miss invokes its loader once.
Only cache implementation and focused tests may change.

relevantContracts.title: Cache entries remain tenant isolated
relevantContracts.invariant: Canonical cache keys include tenantId before resourceId while TTL and miss behavior remain stable.

irrelevantContracts.title: Metrics labels remain bounded
irrelevantContracts.invariant: Metrics never use raw resource ids as labels.
